findings.excerpt: §12.4 Indemnification. Customer shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless nCino and its affiliates from any and all third-party claims arising out of or relating to Customer's use of the Risk-AI Scoring Engine, including without limitation any claims of unlawful discrimination, disparate impact, or violation of consumer-protection statutes, regardless of whether such claims allege defects in the underlying Tool.

highlights.term: regardless of whether such claims allege defects in the underlying Tool

void_clause.explanation: Purports to indemnify the developer (nCino) from its own anti-discrimination liability arising from defects in the Tool. SB 26-189 §1707(7) renders such provisions void as against public policy. Renegotiate before the AG cure window closes.

findings.excerpt: Exhibit B, §2. The Pre-Qualification Optimizer will evaluate, score, and personalize offer terms for each applicant based on predicted conversion probability and lifetime value, drawing on credit-bureau soft pulls and demographic aggregates provided by Customer.

Schedule 1, §4(a). The FraudShield service automatically flags applications for manual fraud review using machine-learning models trained on device-fingerprint, identity-signal, and behavioral inputs. The Customer remains responsible for any approve/deny decisions made on the basis of such flags.

§9.3 Indemnity. Customer shall indemnify and hold iCIMS harmless from any and all claims, demands, or losses relating to the Video-Interview Sentiment Tool, including but not limited to claims under federal, state, or local anti-discrimination law and any biometric-information privacy law, regardless of the cause of such claim.

highlights.term: claims under federal, state, or local anti-discrimination law
regardless of the cause of such claim

void_clause.explanation: Purports to indemnify the developer (iCIMS) from its own anti-discrimination liability. SB 26-189 §1707(7) renders such provisions void; BIPA indemnity language is separately unenforceable under 740 ILCS 14/. Renegotiate at next renewal — and earlier if AG inquiry hits.

findings.excerpt: Exhibit A, §1.2. The HireVue Assessment output may substantially assist or replace discretionary candidate decisions made by Customer's recruiters. Customer acknowledges that the Tool produces a recommendation, not a final decision, and that human review of borderline candidates is the Customer's responsibility.

highlights.term: substantially assist or replace discretionary candidate decisions

findings.excerpt: §3.1 Tool Function. Skills-Match shall rank, score, and prioritize candidates against a job requisition using resume content, extracted skills, and tenure history. Output controls the order in which candidates are surfaced to recruiters for first-pass review.

findings.contract_name: Smart Eye Driver Monitoring License Agreement
findings.excerpt: §14.2 Liability and Indemnity. Licensee shall indemnify, defend, and hold Smart Eye harmless from any and all claims arising from Licensee's deployment of the driver-monitoring system, including without limitation claims of bias, discrimination, or violation of biometric-information privacy law, even where such claims allege defects in the Tool's underlying facial-recognition models.

highlights.term: indemnify, defend, and hold Smart Eye harmless
claims of bias, discrimination, or violation of biometric-information privacy law
even where such claims allege defects in the Tool's underlying facial-recognition models

void_clause.explanation: Purports to indemnify the developer (Smart Eye) from its own anti-discrimination and BIPA liability arising from defects in the facial-recognition models. SB 26-189 §1707(7) voids the SB 26-189 portion; IL BIPA §15 separately bars BIPA-claim indemnity. Two regimes, one void clause — renegotiate before Aria EV launch.

findings.excerpt: Exhibit C, §2. The AI Valuation Engine personalizes trade-in offer values for each consumer based on KBB book value, regional demand signals, and (where Customer has authorized telematics data sharing) behavioral-wear signals derived from connected-vehicle telemetry.

highlights.term: behavioral-wear signals derived from connected-vehicle telemetry

findings.contract_name: Pinetop MAC Core — Captive Finance Model Agreement (internal)
findings.excerpt: Schedule II, §3. The MAC Core model shall evaluate, score, and recommend approve/deny decisions for captive auto-loan applications, using credit-bureau data, application data, dealer-submitted information, and connected-vehicle telematics from prior Pinetop vehicles owned by the applicant.

highlights.term: evaluate, score, and recommend approve/deny decisions
connected-vehicle telematics from prior Pinetop vehicles
